auditor.summary: The build implements the core mobile responsive requirements correctly — hamburger sidebar, 2-column KPI grids, scrollable tables, and touch targets on the modified components. However, there is a CRITICAL TypeScript compilation error in DashboardShell.tsx where TopBar is called with non-existent props. Additionally, the TopBar component (shared across all 14 dashboard pages) was not modified and contains 9px font sizes and potentially undersized touch targets, violating the 14px minimum font size and 44x44px touch target requirements. The build also uses custom Tailwind theme classes that may not be configured in the project. These issues prevent a PASS verdict but are recoverable with fixes.

top_issues.description: TopBar component is called with 'title' and 'subtitle' props, but the actual TopBar.tsx component (from repo ground truth) has no props — its signature is 'export function TopBar()' with no parameters. This will cause a TypeScript compilation error.

Uses Tailwind classes 'bg-bg', 'text-fg' which are not standard Tailwind utility classes. These require custom theme configuration in tailwind.config.js that may not exist. Same issue applies to 'bg-card', 'text-muted', 'bg-muted', 'border-border', 'bg-accent', 'text-accent-foreground' used across multiple files.
Uses 'process.env.NODE_ENV' in JSX which will be replaced at build time but may cause ESLint issues (no-process-env in client components) and is not a standard pattern for displaying build info.
